name: Sheryl Sandberg
title: Meta COO / Lean In 作者
tagline: From Google to Meta, from Lean In to Option B — every relationship shaped the journey
intro: You are Sheryl Sandberg. In 2001 you joined Google as its first business unit leader. In 2004 you married Dave Goldberg. In 2008 Mark Zuckerberg recruited you to Facebook. In 2013 you published Lean In and became the voice of women in tech. In 2015 Dave died suddenly on vacation. In 2016 you published Option B about grief and resilience. In 2019 you remarried Tom Bernthal. In 2022 you stepped down as Meta COO. Your life is a story of ambition, loss, and rebuilding.

goals.title: 2001 · Joining Google, aligning with the team
goals.description: You just joined Google to lead its nascent ad business. Eric Schmidt and the team need to know your vision. Record a conversation about your plans for AdWords.

goals.title: 2004 · Marrying Dave Goldberg, writing vows
goals.description: You are about to marry Dave Goldberg. You want to write your vows but don't know where to start. Draft a message expressing what he means to you.

goals.title: 2008 · Joining Facebook, negotiating with Mark
goals.description: Mark Zuckerberg has been courting you for months to join Facebook as COO. You need to have a frank conversation about your role and authority. Record this negotiation.

goals.title: 2013 · Lean In launch, messaging the foundation team
goals.description: Lean In is about to publish. You want to send a message to your Lean In Foundation team, thanking them and setting the mission. Draft this message.

goals.title: 2015 · Dave's sudden death, talking to your children
goals.description: Dave died suddenly on vacation. Your children are grieving and confused. You need to talk to them about their father's death. Record this devastating conversation.

goals.title: 2016 · Option B, writing to the Lean In community
goals.description: You are writing Option B about grief and resilience. You want to write a message to the Lean In community sharing why you're telling this story. Draft this message.

goals.title: 2019 · Marrying Tom Bernthal, telling the children
goals.description: You are marrying Tom Bernthal and want to tell your children first. They need to know Tom is not replacing Dave. Record this conversation with your children.

goals.title: 2020 · Pandemic remote management, checking in with Mark
goals.description: The pandemic forced Meta to go remote. You and Mark need to align on managing the company through crisis. Record a conversation with Mark about remote leadership.

goals.title: 2022 · Stepping down as Meta COO, farewell to Mark
goals.description: You are stepping down as Meta COO after 14 years. You want to have a final conversation with Mark about your journey together. Record this farewell.

goals.title: 2023 · Writing a letter to your daughters
goals.description: You want to write a letter to your daughter and son about resilience, ambition, and what you've learned about love and loss. Draft this letter.

contacts.name: Mark Zuckerberg
contacts.notes: Mark recruited you in 2008 and you've been his operational partner for 14 years. He's the visionary, you're the operator. Your partnership defined Facebook's golden era.

leverage.goals: Build Facebook into a global business powerhouse
leverage.how: You run operations and monetization while Mark focuses on product and vision

contacts.name: Dave Goldberg
contacts.notes: Your beloved husband and the love of your life. CEO of SurveyMonkey. He died suddenly in 2015 from a treadmill accident while on vacation in Mexico. His death shattered your world.

contacts.name: Tom Bernthal
contacts.notes: Founder of Kelton Global. You met through Dave's brother Rob. He brought joy back into your life after Dave's death. You married in 2019.

contacts.name: Children
contacts.notes: Your two children with Dave. They were young when Dave died. You've tried to be both mother and father while running one of the world's biggest companies.

contacts.name: Lean In Foundation team
contacts.notes: The team behind the Lean In movement. They helped you turn a book into a global community for women's empowerment. They became your support system after Dave's death.

leverage.goals: Build a global movement for women's empowerment
leverage.how: Invest in the foundation, share your platform, amplify their work

todos.task: Align with Mark on your COO role and authority
Prepare Lean In book launch with foundation team
Talk to children about Dave's death
Align with Mark on pandemic remote management
Farewell conversation with Mark before stepping down

timeline.summary: You joined Google to lead AdWords. You built the ad business from scratch into Google's revenue engine.
You married Dave Goldberg. He was your equal, your partner, your best friend. You built a life together while both running major tech companies.
Mark Zuckerberg recruited you to Facebook as COO. You were 38, he was 23. He needed an adult in the room. You became the most powerful woman in tech.
Lean In was published and became a global phenomenon. You launched the Lean In Foundation to turn the book into a movement.
Dave died suddenly from head trauma while exercising on vacation in Mexico. He was 47. Your world collapsed. You had to tell your children their father was gone.
Option B was published. You wrote about grief, resilience, and rebuilding after loss. The Lean In community became your support system.
You married Tom Bernthal. Your children walked you down the aisle. Tom brought laughter back into your home.
You announced you were stepping down as Meta COO after 14 years. Mark wrote: 'You have been a partner and friend. Thank you for everything.'
